title: eriosolena pendula
intro: Eriosolena pendula, commonly known as the hanging golden bell, is a flowering plant species in the family Acanthaceae. It is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The therapeutic actions of Eriosolena pendula include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antiseptic. The bioactive constituents of the plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties and are responsible for its therapeutic actions.
side_effects: Eriosolena pendula can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and dizziness due to its high concentration of alkaloids. Over-ingestion may also lead to headaches, tremors, and abdominal pain in some individuals. In severe cases, improper use of Eriosolena pendula can cause respiratory failure, cardiac arrest, and even death. Additionally, long-term misuse can result in damage to the liver and kidneys, further exacerbating health complications.
